generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in because a back tooth never came through and there was a hard lump in the jaw.

Then the X-ray loaded 😳

And a dense knot of something was packed into the bone blocking the way.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What is the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Complex odontoma

👀 What you see:
A dense irregular knot of tooth material with a thin dark rim packed into the jaw and blocking a molar from coming through.

💊 Treatment:
It is removed and the blocked tooth can then come in.

🦴 Why it matters:
An odontoma is a benign growth of dental tissue but it can trap a tooth and stop it erupting until it is taken out.
generated.ctaText:
If a knot of teeth packed into someone's jaw made you look twice.

I pulled every weird dental and tooth case I have into one PDF.

Impacted teeth, extra teeth, teeth in places they should never be.

Support the page if you'd like and I'd appreciate it 🙏
